about.text:
Creating, programming, and designing are some of the things that make programming and the world of technology in general seem very interesting to me. I enjoy working on projects on my own, learning and improving my programming skills, and staying up-to-date with news related to my field.

I am ready to face any challenge.

skills.tech-1: Frontend - HTML, CSS, Javascript/Typescript, React (NextJS) and Angular
skills.tech-2: Backend - NodeJS, Django and Spring boot
skills.tech-3: Databases - MSSQL, MySQL and SAP Hana
skills.tech-4: SAP Business One - VB.NET and C#
skills.personal-3: Teamwork and good relation with partners

projects.description-1:
GPT-Assistant is a website with a simple intelligent assistant that helps you to translate words or phrases in different languages, and generates recipes depending on what you write in the text box. This assistant is basic but every developer can add a task or utility in this project.

The project is divided in two main parts, which are the server side and the client side. I use React with react router, NodeJS (especifically ExpressJS) and the OpenAI API for the text and image generation.
projects.description-2:
Entertaining Media is a website where you can search all kind of contents to watch, read or play. Its slogan is "What's your next thing to fight against boredom?", because in here you can find everything to get entertained in different ways. All the contents shown in this page are fetched from different APIs, such as TMDB (for movies), IGDB (for games) and Google Books API.

The whole project is made in the current lastest version of NextJS (v.14) for routing and server side rendering in most of the React components.
projects.description-3:
This application is about a web page where you can write notes and organize them in your own way.

The project is divided into two parts: The 'front-end' part is done with React with Typescript, and the 'back-end' part is done with Django (this one manages requests for the MySQL database).
projects.more: Look at other projects in my GitHub!
